problemStatement: Agent Olympus orchestrates work effectively but lacks disciplinary guardrails that prevent common AI-agent failure modes: writing code before design validation, skipping tests, declaring done without verification evidence, and applying fixes before understanding root causes. The Superpowers plugin encodes these disciplines but lacks AO's orchestration power. Merging methodologies creates a single plugin with both discipline AND orchestration.
targetUsers: Solo AI developers using Claude Code with Agent Olympus for autonomous task completion; team leads running Athena multi-agent teams; developers debugging failures during Atlas/Athena execution.
goals: Embed TDD (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) as non-skippable gate in Atlas Phase 3 and Athena Phase 2
Add brainstorm-first gate for complex/architectural tasks
Replace trial-and-error debugging with systematic root-cause investigation
Enforce verification-before-completion iron law with machine-verifiable evidence
Implement two-stage code review (spec compliance + code quality)
Deliver 4 new skills and 1 new agent
All 182 existing tests continue to pass
nonGoals: Change plugin hook registration or run.cjs

userStories.title: TDD Skill -- RED-GREEN-REFACTOR Workflow
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /tdd to implement a feature using strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR discipline
userStories.soThat: every feature ships with tests written before production code
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN a user invokes /tdd with a feature description WHEN the skill starts THEN it enters RED phase: writes a failing test first, runs it, confirms the test fails with the expected assertion error
GIVEN the RED phase completes WHEN GREEN phase begins THEN it writes minimum production code, runs tests, confirms previously-failing test now passes
GIVEN GREEN phase completes WHEN REFACTOR phase begins THEN it reviews for duplication, applies refactoring, re-runs full test suite, confirms zero regressions
GIVEN any phase fails WHEN error is detected THEN the skill diagnoses and fixes within that phase before advancing
GIVEN REFACTOR phase passes WHEN cycle completes THEN outputs summary: test file path, production file path, test count before/after, phase durations

userStories.title: TDD Gate in Atlas Phase 3 Execution
userStories.iWantTo: Atlas to enforce TDD discipline on every implementation story
userStories.soThat: no story is marked passes:true without a test written and passing first
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Atlas Phase 3 executes a claude story WHEN executor is prompted THEN prompt includes TDD instruction
GIVEN a story executor completes WHEN Atlas verifies THEN checks at least one NEW test exists via git diff
GIVEN executor produces code but zero new tests WHEN detected THEN Atlas rejects story, spawns test-engineer, marks passes:true only after both pass
GIVEN a Codex worker completes WHEN Atlas verifies THEN same test-existence check applies

userStories.title: TDD Gate in Athena Phase 2 Worker Prompts
userStories.iWantTo: every Athena worker to receive TDD instructions in spawn prompt
userStories.soThat: parallel workers independently follow test-first discipline
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Athena spawns a Claude worker WHEN prompt is constructed THEN includes TDD instruction
GIVEN Athena spawns a Codex worker WHEN tmux prompt is constructed THEN includes TDD instruction
GIVEN a worker completes and Phase 4 integrates WHEN merge verification runs THEN checks each branch contains test file changes
GIVEN a worker branch contains zero test changes WHEN detected THEN spawns test-engineer to backfill

userStories.title: Brainstorm Skill -- Design-Before-Code Gate
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /brainstorm to explore design alternatives before implementation
userStories.soThat: complex features have a validated design before any code is written
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN user invokes /brainstorm WHEN skill starts THEN generates 3 distinct design alternatives with pros, cons, effort, risk
GIVEN 3 alternatives generated WHEN presented THEN highlights trade-offs in comparison table and recommends one
GIVEN user selects approach WHEN finalized THEN writes design brief to .ao/brainstorm-<slug>.md
GIVEN user invokes /brainstorm then /atlas or /athena WHEN orchestrator starts THEN detects brainstorm artifact and injects into planning

userStories.title: Brainstorm Gate in Atlas/Athena for Complex Tasks
userStories.iWantTo: Atlas and Athena to auto-invoke brainstorm for complex/architectural tasks
userStories.soThat: the system never jumps to code on hard problems without exploring alternatives
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Atlas classifies task as complex/architectural WHEN no brainstorm artifact exists THEN invokes brainstorm skill
GIVEN Athena classifies task as complex/architectural WHEN no brainstorm artifact exists THEN invokes brainstorm skill
GIVEN brainstorm completes WHEN orchestrator resumes THEN chosen design passed as design_context to planning
GIVEN task is trivial/moderate WHEN triage completes THEN brainstorm NOT invoked

userStories.title: Systematic Debug Skill
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /systematic-debug to follow structured investigation protocol
userStories.soThat: root causes are identified before any fix is attempted
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN user invokes /systematic-debug WHEN skill starts THEN enters INVESTIGATE phase: reproduces error, collects stack trace, identifies file:line
GIVEN INVESTIGATE completes WHEN HYPOTHESIZE phase begins THEN generates 3 ranked hypotheses with predicted evidence
GIVEN hypotheses ranked WHEN PROBE phase begins THEN executes top hypothesis confirmation check, reports confirmed/denied with evidence
GIVEN root cause confirmed WHEN FIX phase begins THEN applies minimal fix, re-runs failing test, confirms pass
GIVEN fix passes WHEN VERIFY phase begins THEN runs full test suite, confirms zero regressions
GIVEN user invokes without error description WHEN skill starts THEN reads last 50 lines of terminal to auto-detect error

userStories.title: Systematic Debug Integration in Debugger Agent
userStories.iWantTo: debugger agent to follow systematic investigation protocol
userStories.soThat: it stops trial-and-error and finds root causes first
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN debugger agent receives fix request WHEN it starts THEN persona mandates root-cause-first protocol
GIVEN debugger invoked by Atlas/Athena Phase 4 WHEN error output provided THEN response includes: confirmed root cause, evidence, minimal fix, full test result
GIVEN debugger cannot confirm root cause after 3 hypotheses WHEN all denied THEN escalates to trace skill

userStories.title: Trace Skill Enhancement -- Systematic Debug Protocol
userStories.iWantTo: trace skill to use systematic debug methodology in investigation lanes
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN trace Phase 2 spawns lanes WHEN debugger prompted THEN includes systematic debug protocol instruction
GIVEN trace Phase 3 rebuttal completes WHEN probe identified THEN spec includes exact command, expected output if correct, expected output if wrong
GIVEN trace Phase 4 probe confirms cause WHEN Phase 5 fix begins THEN fix agent receives full evidence chain

userStories.title: Verification-Before-Completion Iron Law
userStories.iWantTo: Atlas and Athena to enforce no-done-without-evidence rule
userStories.soThat: I never encounter done tasks that are actually broken
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Atlas Phase 4 VERIFY passes WHEN checkpoint saved THEN includes verificationEvidence with buildLog, testOutput, lintResult
GIVEN Athena Phase 4 INTEGRATE passes WHEN checkpoint saved THEN includes same verificationEvidence
GIVEN Phase 5 REVIEW approved WHEN completion report generated THEN includes verificationEvidence block
GIVEN ambiguous verification output WHEN evaluated THEN treated as FAILED, re-runs with explicit count extraction
GIVEN story about to be marked passes:true WHEN verification runs THEN requires: test file exists, 0 failures, build exit code 0

userStories.title: Finish-Branch Skill -- Pre-Merge Checklist
userStories.asA: developer working on a feature branch
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /finish-branch to run complete pre-merge checklist
userStories.soThat: I never merge with failing tests, missing verification, or incomplete reviews
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN user invokes /finish-branch WHEN skill starts THEN runs: test suite, build, lint, verify-coverage, two-stage review
GIVEN all 5 checks pass WHEN skill completes THEN outputs summary: branch, commits, files changed, test results, coverage gaps, review verdict, GO/NO-GO
GIVEN any check fails WHEN detected THEN stops at that check with actionable detail, does NOT proceed (fail-fast)
GIVEN user on main branch WHEN detected THEN warns and requires explicit confirmation
GIVEN GO recommendation and user confirms WHEN proceeding THEN offers to create merge commit or PR

userStories.title: Two-Stage Code Review in Atlas/Athena Phase 5
userStories.iWantTo: two-stage review: spec compliance first, code quality second
userStories.soThat: reviews catch both wrong-feature and bad-code problems
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Atlas Phase 5 begins WHEN reviewers spawned THEN Stage 1 runs first: spec-compliance check per story
GIVEN Stage 1 all COMPLIANT WHEN Stage 2 begins THEN existing reviewers run in parallel (current behavior preserved)
GIVEN Stage 1 finds NON-COMPLIANT WHEN verdict returned THEN executor fixes non-compliance BEFORE Stage 2
GIVEN Athena Phase 5 begins WHEN same protocol applies THEN behavior matches Atlas

userStories.title: Quality-Gate Agent
userStories.iWantTo: a quality-gate agent that verifies story completion with machine evidence
userStories.soThat: verification is consistent and not dependent on individual agent interpretation
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN agents/quality-gate.md created WHEN receives story ID and criteria THEN runs each criterion as specific check with PASS/FAIL and evidence
GIVEN quality-gate reports FAIL WHEN returned to orchestrator THEN knows exactly which criterion failed for targeted fix
GIVEN unverifiable criterion encountered WHEN detected THEN flags as MANUAL_REVIEW_NEEDED, does not block pipeline
GIVEN model tier assigned WHEN frontmatter set THEN model is sonnet

userStories.title: Code-Reviewer Agent Enrichment
userStories.iWantTo: code-reviewer to be aware of spec compliance context
userStories.soThat: it distinguishes spec-mismatch from quality issues
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN code-reviewer receives prompt with specContext WHEN reviewing THEN prioritizes spec-noncompliance findings as CRITICAL
GIVEN code-reviewer receives prompt WITHOUT specContext WHEN reviewing THEN falls back to current behavior
GIVEN checklist updated WHEN read THEN includes 'Spec alignment' item

userStories.title: Debugger Agent Enrichment -- Mandatory Root-Cause Protocol
userStories.iWantTo: debugger agent to have explicit ban on trial-and-error
userStories.soThat: every debug session produces root-cause analysis before code change
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN agents/debugger.md updated WHEN read THEN contains IRON RULE: must confirm root cause BEFORE writing any fix
GIVEN process section updated WHEN read THEN follows 5-step: Reproduce, Hypothesize(3), Probe, Fix, Verify
GIVEN process section updated WHEN read THEN includes escalation to trace if all 3 hypotheses denied

userStories.title: Verify-Coverage Enhancement -- Shallow Coverage Detection
userStories.iWantTo: verify-coverage to report shallow coverage, not just missing tests
userStories.soThat: coverage gaps include insufficient test depth
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN test file exists but has <2 test cases for file with 3+ exports WHEN mapped THEN flagged as SHALLOW COVERAGE
GIVEN gap report generated WHEN rendered THEN includes Shallow Coverage section
GIVEN skill completes WHEN summary rendered THEN includes: total files, fully covered, shallow, missing, percentage

userStories.title: Plan Skill Enhancement -- Brainstorm Integration
userStories.iWantTo: plan to invoke brainstorm automatically for L-scale tasks
userStories.soThat: large plans have a validated design foundation
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN L-scale detected WHEN no brainstorm artifact exists THEN invokes brainstorm between Phase 0 and Phase 1
GIVEN brainstorm completes WHEN Phase 1 begins THEN output passed as design_context to Hermes
GIVEN S or M scale WHEN Phase 0 completes THEN brainstorm NOT invoked

userStories.title: Consensus-Plan TDD Story Tagging
userStories.iWantTo: consensus-plan to tag stories with requiresTDD
userStories.soThat: executors know which stories need test-first discipline
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN Phase 1 DRAFT runs WHEN stories generated THEN each includes requiresTDD boolean
GIVEN PRD written WHEN requiresTDD present THEN Atlas/Athena include TDD instructions only for requiresTDD:true stories
GIVEN story has requiresTDD:true WHEN completes THEN quality-gate verifies new test exists

userStories.title: Superpowers Coexistence Detection
userStories.iWantTo: AO to detect whether Superpowers is also installed
userStories.soThat: it can optionally delegate to Superpowers skills when both present
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN AO starts session WHEN initialization runs THEN checks for Superpowers skill names in Skill registry
GIVEN Superpowers NOT installed WHEN AO operates THEN all methodology is self-contained (standalone, no errors)
GIVEN Superpowers IS installed WHEN TDD skill invoked THEN MAY invoke superpowers:tdd, falling back to own implementation on failure
GIVEN detection runs WHEN result cached THEN runs at most once per session

constraints: Zero npm dependencies -- Node.js built-ins only
ESM only (.mjs) for all new scripts
182 existing tests must pass after integration
AO must work standalone without Superpowers
File permissions: 0o600 for state, 0o700 for dirs
Greek mythology naming convention for agents

openQuestions.question: Should brainstorm auto-select in autonomous mode or always be interactive?
openQuestions.recommendedDefault: Auto-select in autonomous mode, interactive on direct invocation
openQuestions.impactAnalysis: If always interactive: blocks autonomous execution. If always auto: user loses design input.

openQuestions.question: Should TDD gate apply to Codex workers or only Claude?
openQuestions.recommendedDefault: Apply to both, verify after completion for Codex
openQuestions.impactAnalysis: Codex-only skip: inconsistent. Strict enforcement: may cause Codex failures.

openQuestions.question: Should quality-gate be a standalone skill or internal-only agent?
openQuestions.impactAnalysis: If also a skill: useful for manual verification but adds maintenance.

openQuestions.question: Minimum test count per story for TDD gate?
openQuestions.recommendedDefault: 1 new test case minimum per requiresTDD:true story
openQuestions.impactAnalysis: Higher minimum: more thorough but may over-constrain small stories.
